rationale: XRP has a fixed maximum supply of 100 billion tokens, all of which were pre-mined at genesis with no new issuance mechanism or mining rewards; the only supply dynamic is deflationary, as a small amount of XRP is permanently destroyed (burned) as transaction fees on the XRPL (currently 10 drops = 0.00001 XRP per transaction). Circulating supply changes reflect Ripple's periodic escrow releases and sales, but these do not constitute new issuance — they are distributions from the pre-existing fixed pool held in escrow, making net new inflation effectively zero (and technically slightly negative due to fee burns).
